data.title: Immunotech Biopharm Subsidiary Signs RMB9.75 Million Cell Therapy Service Deal With Tasly
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Immunotech Biopharm Ltd ( (HK:6978) ) has shared an update.

Immunotech Biopharm Ltd, a Hong Kong-listed biopharmaceutical company, develops and processes cell therapies and related services, leveraging its subsidiary Yongtai Beijing’s GMP-compliant facilities and technical expertise in cell product processing and quality control. The company announced that Yongtai Beijing has entered into a roughly 24‑month entrustment service agreement with connected party Tasly to provide cell product thawing, washing, formulation, filling, testing and project management services for a clinical project, for a service fee of RMB9.75 million subject to adjustment, with the deal classified as a continuing connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules that requires reporting, annual review and announcement but is exempt from independent shareholders’ approval due to its size, signaling incremental service revenue and a deeper operational tie with a substantial shareholder.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6978) stock is a Hold with a HK$2.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Immunotech Biopharm Ltd stock, see the HK:6978 Stock Forecast page.

More about Immunotech Biopharm Ltd

Immunotech Biopharm Ltd is a Hong Kong-listed biopharmaceutical company focused on cell therapy, operating through subsidiaries such as Yongtai Beijing, which provides cell product processing and quality control services using GMP-compliant facilities and established quality management systems.

An update from Scholar Education Group ( (HK:1769) ) is now available.

Scholar Education Group has announced that the trustee of its share award scheme purchased 1,500,000 shares on the market on 27 January 2026, representing approximately 0.223% of its issued share capital, at an average price of about HK$2.06 per share and a total consideration of roughly HK$3.09 million. Following this transaction, the trustee holds 15,050,000 shares, or around 2.23% of the company’s issued share capital, and the board stated that the current share price significantly undervalues the company’s underlying value, signaling confidence in its business outlook and an intention to continue monitoring market conditions and directing further purchases for future share awards to selected participants under the scheme.

data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Semiconductor Manufacturing International ( (HK:0981) ) just unveiled an update.

Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation has called an extraordinary general meeting in Shanghai on 12 February 2026 to seek shareholder approval for a proposed asset purchase to be executed through the issuance of new shares and related cash consideration. The meeting will vote on a series of ordinary resolutions covering the overall transaction plan, share issuance terms, lock-up arrangements, profit and loss attribution for the target asset during the transition period, and the transfer of ownership, as well as confirming that the deal complies with multiple PRC and stock exchange regulations governing major asset restructurings and related-party transactions. Shareholders will also consider a supplemental agreement with counterparties and a report on the asset purchase, along with confirmations that the transaction does not constitute a major asset restructuring or reorganization under applicable rules, and a review of the company’s asset purchases and sales over the preceding 12 months, underscoring SMIC’s effort to structure the deal within strict regulatory frameworks and enhance transparency for investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0981) stock is a Hold with a HK$79.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Semiconductor Manufacturing International stock, see the HK:0981 Stock Forecast page.

More about Semiconductor Manufacturing International

Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation (SMIC) is a leading semiconductor foundry engaged in the manufacturing and sale of integrated circuit wafers and related services, serving global chip design companies and electronics manufacturers, with a strategic focus on the Chinese and international semiconductor markets.

data.title: Smart Globe Forecasts Profit Turnaround on Surging Africa-Focused Logistics Revenue
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Smart Globe Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1481) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Smart Globe Holdings Limited has issued a positive profit alert, indicating it expects to swing from a net loss of about HK$12 million in 2024 to a net profit of between HK$10 million and HK$16 million for the year ended 31 December 2025. The turnaround is driven by a sharp rise in revenue from its supply chain management services, projected at HK$108 million to HK$114 million versus HK$9.5 million a year earlier, on the back of strong logistics demand in the Sub-Saharan Africa market, partly offset by a decline in its printing segment revenue to HK$90 million to HK$96 million from HK$108.1 million. The figures are based on unaudited management accounts and may differ from the final audited results, but the guidance signals a significant shift in the group’s earnings profile towards higher-growth logistics and supply chain services and away from its legacy printing operations.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1481) stock is a Hold with a HK$3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Smart Globe Holdings Ltd. stock, see the HK:1481 Stock Forecast page.

More about Smart Globe Holdings Ltd.

Smart Globe Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates through two main segments: supply chain management services, with a particular focus on logistics, and a traditional printing business. The company has recently expanded its market reach by tapping into demand from the Sub-Saharan Africa region, especially for its logistics-related services within the supply chain management segment.

Star Group Company Limited ( (HK:1560) ) just unveiled an update.

Star Group Asia Limited has issued a supplemental announcement regarding its previously disclosed disposal of certain properties and the related leaseback arrangement, clarifying how the leaseback is classified under Hong Kong listing rules. The company states that, as the relevant percentage ratios for the leaseback exceed 5% but remain below 25%, the transaction is treated as a discloseable transaction subject only to reporting and announcement requirements, without the need for a shareholder circular or approval. The board reiterates that the disposal and leaseback terms, including the consideration, are on normal commercial terms, fair and reasonable, and in the interests of the company and its shareholders as a whole, signalling management’s view that the transaction appropriately balances capital management with continued operational use of the properties.

data.description:

Clifford Modern Living Holdings Ltd ( (HK:3686) ) has shared an announcement.

Clifford Modern Living Holdings Limited has redeployed part of the RMB387.9 million proceeds from its earlier disposal of 680,000 ounces of unallocated silver bullion into gold, placing two separate orders for a total of 1,600 ounces of unallocated gold bullion through a bank. The first 800-ounce purchase, valued at about HK$31.9 million, was ordered on 26 January 2026 and is expected to complete on 28 January 2026, while the second 800-ounce tranche, worth approximately HK$31.5 million and ordered on 27 January 2026, is slated to settle on 29 January 2026. Together, the two gold investments qualify as a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong Listing Rules, triggering reporting and announcement obligations but not requiring shareholder approval, underscoring the company’s ongoing rebalancing of its precious metals portfolio and its use of commodity investments as a capital management tool without altering its control structure or governance obligations to investors.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Legend Upstar Holdings Limited ( (HK:0459) ) has provided an announcement.

Legend Upstar Holdings Limited has called an extraordinary general meeting of shareholders in Hong Kong on 20 February 2026 to vote on a proposed scheme of arrangement that will fundamentally alter its share capital structure. The proposal involves cancelling and extinguishing a defined class of scheme shares, followed by restoring the company’s issued share capital by issuing an equivalent number of new shares at par value to the designated offeror, with the resultant capital credit used to fully pay up those new shares; directors are also seeking authority to implement the scheme, agree to any court-imposed modifications and apply for the withdrawal of the company’s share listing from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, signalling a potential take-private transaction with significant implications for existing shareholders and the company’s public-market status.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0459) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.13 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Legend Upstar Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:0459 Stock Forecast page.

Legend Upstar Holdings Limited ( (HK:0459) ) has shared an announcement.

Legend Upstar Holdings Limited has convened a court-ordered meeting of its scheme shareholders in Hong Kong to vote on a proposed scheme of arrangement under the Companies Act of the Cayman Islands. The meeting, chaired by an independent non-executive director, will determine whether shareholders approve the transaction framework set out in a composite scheme document, with voting procedures allowing for personal or proxy participation and specific arrangements for joint and beneficial holders. If the scheme is approved by shareholders, it will still require sanction from the Grand Court of the Cayman Islands, marking a key legal and procedural step that could materially reshape the company’s capital structure or ownership and carries significant implications for shareholders depending on the scheme’s ultimate terms and court outcome.

data.title: MMG Flags US$280–300 Million Kinsevere Impairment but Maintains Strong 2025 Profit Outlook
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

MMG ( (HK:1208) ) has shared an update.

MMG Limited has warned that its 2025 results will include a non-cash impairment charge of approximately US$280 million to US$300 million tied to its Kinsevere operation in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, following a review that identified issues such as cobalt sales limitations, power-related production disruptions, ramp-up and operational challenges, and fiscal regime uncertainties. Despite the write-down and headwinds including power interruptions at Kinsevere, ongoing cobalt export restrictions and hedging losses, the miner still expects to report a substantial unaudited net profit after tax attributable to equity holders of between US$500 million and US$520 million for 2025, underpinned by strong operating performances at Las Bambas and Dugald River and elevated prices for copper and precious metals, with the impairment having no impact on the group’s cash flow.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1208) stock is a Hold with a HK$11.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on MMG stock, see the HK:1208 Stock Forecast page.

More about MMG

MMG Limited is a Hong Kong–incorporated resources company focused on the exploration, development and mining of base metals, notably copper and cobalt, with key operations including the Las Bambas mine in Peru, the Dugald River zinc operation in Australia and the Kinsevere copper operation in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

An announcement from Tianjin Development Holdings ( (HK:0882) ) is now available.

Tianjin Development Holdings has agreed to dispose of its entire equity interest in Benefo Financial Leasing Co., Ltd. through its 82.74%-owned subsidiary Tianjin Tai Kang, which will sell 100% of Benefo Financial Leasing to Tianjin Capital for RMB264.93 million (approximately HK$294.37 million), based on an asset-based valuation as at 30 September 2025 and in compliance with PRC state-owned asset transfer rules. Following completion and the required regulatory and state-owned asset approvals, Benefo Financial Leasing will cease to be a subsidiary of the group, with Tianjin Capital assuming all profits and losses from 30 September 2025 to completion, marking an adjustment to Tianjin Development’s portfolio exposure to financial leasing while meeting Hong Kong Listing Rules disclosure requirements for a discloseable transaction.

data.title: Nanhua Futures’ US Unit Gains Trading Membership on Nodal Exchange
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nanhua Futures Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2691) ) just unveiled an update.

Nanhua Futures Co., Ltd. announced that its indirect wholly owned subsidiary, Nanhua USA LLC, has been approved as a trading member of Nodal Exchange, a leading North American derivatives exchange focused on power, natural gas and environmental markets. Nanhua USA, already a clearing member of Nodal Clear, can now both trade and clear products listed on Nodal Exchange, strengthening the group’s capabilities and access in North American energy and environmental derivatives and potentially enhancing its international business development, while the company urges shareholders and investors to exercise caution when dealing in its securities.

More about Nanhua Futures Co., Ltd. Class H

Nanhua Futures Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated futures brokerage and derivatives services provider, listed in Hong Kong and operating through subsidiaries including Nanhua USA LLC. The group focuses on futures and derivatives trading and clearing, serving investors with access to major commodity and financial markets, and is expanding its international footprint, particularly in North American energy and environmental derivatives.

data.title: China Longyuan Power Raises RMB2 Billion via Ultra Short-Term Debentures
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from China Longyuan Power Group ( (HK:0916) ) is now available.

China Longyuan Power Group has completed the issuance of RMB2.0 billion in ultra short-term debentures, a 267-day instrument with a coupon rate of 1.55% and a nominal value of RMB100 per unit, with interest accruing from 27 January 2026. Led by China Bohai Bank as lead underwriter and China Minsheng Bank as joint lead underwriter, the proceeds will be used to supplement the company’s daily working capital and repay interest-bearing debt of the company and its subsidiaries, bolstering short-term liquidity and optimizing its debt profile.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0916) stock is a Hold with a HK$7.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Longyuan Power Group stock, see the HK:0916 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Longyuan Power Group

China Longyuan Power Group Corporation Limited is a Hong Kong-listed Chinese power producer, primarily engaged in the development, operation and management of power generation assets, with a focus on the domestic market in the People’s Republic of China. The company raises funds in onshore debt markets to support its operational liquidity and capital structure needs.
